nativeRange.habitat: Coral reefs and rubble areas at depths of roughly 4–65 m; generally observed in pairs or small groups.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical reef-associated marine biotope of the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.

diet.notes: In the wild has been documented feeding on coral polyps, gorgonians, algae, polychaete worms, and small crustaceans. In aquaria generally accepts a varied diet of dried seaweed, Spirulina-based preparations, vitamin-enriched frozen mysis and brine shrimp, and finely chopped meaty foods. Multiple small daily feedings tend to be best.

summary: An attractive Red Sea butterflyfish (Chaetodon paucifasciatus) with a chevron-patterned body and red-edged tail; generally peaceful with other species but best kept singly or as a bonded pair, and not reef-safe.
careNotes: Chaetodon paucifasciatus is endemic to the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden, where it is typically observed in pairs over coral and rubble reef at 4–65 m. In aquaria it has been reported to settle in well when given ample live rock for grazing and shelter plus open swimming space; a tank of around 400 L (roughly 100 US gal) is a sensible cautious minimum even though some retailers list 70 gal, because the species is an active swimmer and territorial toward similar shapes. Generally considered peaceful toward dissimilar tankmates but can be aggressive to conspecifics and other butterflyfishes of similar body shape; best kept singly or as an obligate-monogamous mated pair purchased together. Not reef-safe — has been documented nipping coral polyps, gorgonians, and ornamental worms. Tank-bred specimens are uncommon; most are wild-caught from the Red Sea.
breedingNotes: Oviparous and obligately monogamous in the wild, forming long-term pair bonds during breeding. Captive breeding is not commonly reported.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Retailer husbandry sheet; sourced adult size (5.5"), tank minimum (70 gal), temperament, diet, water parameters (pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, 72–78°F), reef-safe status, compatibility cautions.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Chaetodontidae, Acanthuriformes), max length 14 cm TL, depth 4–65 m, native range Red Sea + Gulf of Aden, wild temperature range 23.6–29.4°C (mean 26.9), diet (coral polyps, gorgonians, algae, worms, crustaceans), monogamous pairing. Used in place of the originally assigned Reef2Reef wiki secondary, which returned HTTP 403.

additional.name: Web search synthesis (LiveAquaria, BlueZoo, theaquariumwiki)
additional.notes: Tertiary cross-check on tank size; sources span 70–100 gal (≈265–379 L). Picked 400 L as the cautious nice-value minimum given active swimming behavior and conspecific aggression.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous broadcast spawner that forms obligate monogamous pairs in the wild; captive breeding is not commonly reported.
